rationale: The cited 2002 paper appears directly relevant to the citing 2013 study’s core phenomenon: both are grocery in-store studies of unplanned buying behavior, and the cited work provides a field experiment/theory on how unexpected coupons drive unplanned purchases. The citing note explicitly models unplanned consideration and conversion with in-store promotion/coupon-related factors, so this citation is plausibly a substantive mechanism/foundation source rather than an incidental mention.
